children.name: Balaeniceps
children.description: A monotypic genus of enormous, stork-like bird with a uniquely massive, shoe-shaped bill that is both its most distinctive feature and its primary hunting tool. The Shoebill is a living fossil, the sole survivor of an ancient lineage of birds. Its slow, deliberate movements and statue-like stillness during hunting have earned it a legendary reputation among African wetland dwellers.

children.name: Balaeniceps rex
children.description: A massive grey bird standing up to 1.5 metres tall, found in the swamps and marshes of South Sudan, Uganda, Tanzania, and Zambia. Its broad, hooked bill is perfectly adapted for catching large fish, particularly lungfish, which it seizes with a lightning-fast lunge. Despite its fearsome appearance, the Shoebill is a patient ambush predator that can stand motionless for hours.
